title: Database Migration Plan Report
description: Produced by db-migration-planner. Consumes the current db-schema.json snapshot and (optionally) a target snapshot of the same shape; produces an ordered migration plan as detected changes + risk-classified, reversibility-tagged steps. Phase 3 ships the contract only — full execution-side support arrives Phase 4+ when migration-applier write-tier agents land. Plan, not verdict; downstream Design / Migration agents consume it to inform decisions.

inputs.description: One entry per db-schema.json snapshot the planner attempted to consume — the `current` snapshot (always expected) and the `target` snapshot (optional; absent when the plan is a structural baseline rather than a diff).

inputs_consumed.description: Count of inputs[] entries with available=true. <= len(inputs).

target_provided.description: True when a `target` input was provided (with or without available=true). Disambiguates the `no_target` planning status from a target that simply failed to load.

planning_status.description: planned = both snapshots loaded and a diff was produced; no_target = current loaded but no target snapshot supplied (structural baseline only); no_current = current snapshot missing or unloadable (cannot diff); skipped = preconditions not met (e.g. db-schema-scanner reported engine=null).

role.description: Which side of the diff this snapshot represents. Exactly one `current` entry is expected; at most one `target`.

generated_at.description: The db-schema.json snapshot's own generated_at. Required when available=true; omitted when available=false.

note.description: Per-input note (e.g. 'snapshot missing', 'engine=null in source report'). Omitted when available=true and the snapshot parsed cleanly.

kind.description: Open-vocabulary category. Common Phase 3 values: table_added, table_removed, column_added, column_removed, column_type_changed, column_nullable_changed, column_default_changed, index_added, index_removed, constraint_added, constraint_removed, relationship_added, relationship_removed, relationship_action_changed, snapshot_invalid.

object.description: Stable identifier of the schema object the change touches — e.g. `users` (table), `users.email` (column), `users_email_key` (index/constraint), `orders.user_id -> users.id` (relationship).

evidence.description: Pointers to specific snapshot fields supporting this change — table names, column tuples, index definitions, constraint kinds. A change without evidence is opinion, not a diff.

order.description: 1-based execution order. Steps are intended to run sequentially; concurrent application is out of scope for this skeleton.

risk.description: low = additive, no data motion (CREATE TABLE, ADD nullable COLUMN, ADD INDEX CONCURRENTLY); medium = additive with backfill or blocking lock (ADD NOT NULL with default, ADD UNIQUE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY); high = destructive or potentially data-losing (DROP COLUMN, narrow type, DROP INDEX with replacement).

reversibility.description: reversible = exact inverse step is safe and complete (DROP TABLE iff just-created, DROP INDEX); irreversible = inverse cannot recover the prior state (DROP COLUMN loses data, type narrowing loses precision).

related_changes.description: Indexes into the top-level changes[] array supporting this step. Empty array means the step is preparatory (e.g. a backup) rather than detected from a diff.

notes.description: Optional per-step note (e.g. 'requires backfill', 'requires application downtime', 'apply during low-traffic window').
